title: Nineteen Eighty-Four
author: George Orwell
description: A totalitarian regime uses surveillance and propaganda to control every aspect of life in a bleak future society.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: A genetically engineered society trades freedom for stability in a pleasure-driven world where individuality is obsolete.

title: The Handmaid's Tale
author: Margaret Atwood
description: In a theocratic dictatorship built on the ruins of America, fertile women are enslaved as reproductive vessels.

title: The Hunger Games
author: Suzanne Collins
description: A teenage girl volunteers to fight to the death in a televised spectacle that keeps a dystopian nation under control.

title: Catching Fire
author: Suzanne Collins
description: The victors of the Hunger Games are forced back into the arena as revolution brews across the districts.

title: The Giver
author: Lois Lowry
description: A boy discovers the dark truth behind his seemingly perfect community where all pain and memory have been eliminated.

title: Divergent
author: Veronica Roth
description: In a society divided into five factions based on virtues, a girl discovers she doesn't fit into any single group.

title: Insurgent
author: Veronica Roth
description: War erupts between the factions as the truth about their society's origins threatens to unravel everything.

title: Allegiant
author: Veronica Roth
description: The faction system collapses as the characters venture beyond the walls to discover what lies outside their city.

title: A Clockwork Orange
author: Anthony Burgess
description: A violent young delinquent undergoes experimental conditioning to cure his criminal impulses in a near-future Britain.

title: The Road
author: Cormac McCarthy
description: A father and son traverse a devastated post-apocalyptic landscape, struggling to survive and preserve their humanity.

title: The Maze Runner
author: James Dashner
description: A group of teens trapped in a deadly maze with no memory must find a way out before time runs out.

title: The Selection
author: Kiera Cass
description: In a future caste-divided society, thirty-five girls compete for the hand of a prince in a televised competition.

title: Parable of the Sower
author: Octavia E. Butler
description: A young woman with hyper-empathy syndrome leads a small community northward through a collapsing America ravaged by climate change.

title: Children of Time
author: Adrian Tchaikovsky
description: The last remnants of humanity encounter an evolved spider civilization on a terraformed world far from a dying Earth.

title: Red Rising
author: Pierce Brown
description: A lowborn miner infiltrates the ruling elite's brutal academy on Mars to spark a revolution from within.

title: Golden Son
author: Pierce Brown
description: A revolutionary disguised as a Gold navigates interplanetary politics and war in the second chapter of the Red Rising saga.

title: Morning Star
author: Pierce Brown
description: The final battle for freedom ignites as the oppressed Colors rise against the Gold overlords of the solar system.

title: Watchmen
author: Alan Moore
description: Retired superheroes investigate a conspiracy that threatens nuclear annihilation in an alternate Cold War America.

title: The Time Machine
author: H. G. Wells
description: A Victorian inventor travels to the distant future and discovers humanity has evolved into two drastically different species.

title: World War Z
author: Max Brooks
description: An oral history of a devastating global zombie pandemic told through survivors' accounts from around the world.

title: Steelheart
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: In a world where superpowered beings have become tyrannical rulers, a young man plots to assassinate the most powerful of them all.

title: The Toll
author: Neal Shusterman
description: In a world where death has been conquered, the Scythes who control population face their greatest threat yet.

title: Sunrise on the Reaping
author: Suzanne Collins
description: A prequel set during the Second Quarter Quell explores the events that shaped the Hunger Games universe.

title: Altered Carbon
author: Richard K. Morgan
description: In a future where consciousness can be transferred between bodies, a former soldier investigates a billionaire's murder.

title: Transmetropolitan Vol. 1
author: Darick Robertson
description: A gonzo journalist returns to a chaotic future city to expose corruption and fight for truth with savage wit.

title: The Dispossessed
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: A physicist from an anarchist moon colony visits the capitalist mother planet, questioning the meaning of freedom and ownership.

title: Ready Player One
author: Ernest Cline
description: In a crumbling near-future, a teenager hunts for an Easter egg hidden inside a massive virtual reality world.

title: Flatland
author: Edwin Abbott Abbott
description: A square living in a two-dimensional world discovers the existence of higher dimensions in this satirical mathematical novella.

title: We
author: Yevgeny Zamyatin
description: A mathematician in a glass-walled totalitarian city begins to question the state after falling for a rebellious woman.

title: Noughts & Crosses
author: Malorie Blackman
description: In an alternate world where dark-skinned Crosses rule over light-skinned Noughts, two teenagers from opposite sides fall in love.

title: Look to Windward
author: Iain Banks
description: A Chelgrian composer visits a Culture orbital on a secret mission of revenge tied to a devastating civil war.

title: Player Piano
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: An engineer questions his role in a fully automated society where machines have replaced nearly all human labor.

title: Island
author: Aldous Huxley
description: A journalist shipwrecked on a utopian island discovers a society that has perfected the balance between science and spirituality.

title: The Will to Battle
author: Ada Palmer
description: In a 25th-century world of flying cars and global governance, a looming war threatens to shatter centuries of peace.

title: Hyperion
author: Dan Simmons
description: Seven pilgrims share their tales as they journey to confront the mysterious Shrike on a distant world at the edge of time.

title: Rendezvous with Rama
author: Arthur C. Clarke
description: A crew explores a massive cylindrical alien starship that enters the solar system, revealing wonders and unanswerable questions.

title: Homo Deus
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping exploration of humanity's future as technology, AI, and biotechnology threaten to redefine what it means to be human.

title: Momo
author: Michael Ende
description: A mysterious girl fights the grey men who steal people's time in this allegorical tale about the value of leisure and imagination.

title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: A woman between life and death visits a library where each book lets her live an alternate version of her life.

title: Binti
author: Nnedi Okorafor
description: A young Himba woman becomes the first of her people to attend an interstellar university and must broker peace with an alien race.

title: The Futurological Congress
author: Stanisław Lem
description: A scientist at a futurology conference discovers that reality itself is being chemically manipulated in this darkly comic satire.

title: Walkaway
author: Cory Doctorow
description: In a world of extreme wealth inequality, people abandon mainstream society to build a post-scarcity commune from the ruins.

title: The Power
author: Naomi Alderman
description: Women develop the ability to generate electrical jolts, upending global power structures and rewriting the history of gender.

title: Klara and the Sun
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: An artificial friend powered by solar energy observes and learns about human love while waiting in a store to be chosen.

title: The Bazaar of Bad Dreams
author: Stephen King
description: A collection of short stories exploring dark futures, moral dilemmas, and the thin line between the mundane and the terrifying.

title: Fahrenheit 451
author: Ray Bradbury
description: A fireman whose job is to burn books begins to question censorship and the mindless entertainment that replaced reading.

title: The Left Hand of Darkness
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: An envoy to a planet of ambisexual beings must navigate alien politics and forge an unlikely alliance in the bitter cold.

title: Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?
author: Philip K. Dick
description: A bounty hunter in a post-apocalyptic San Francisco questions what it means to be human while hunting rogue androids.

title: Neuromancer
author: William Gibson
description: A washed-up computer hacker is hired for one last job—a heist in cyberspace that could reshape artificial intelligence forever.

title: Snow Crash
author: Neal Stephenson
description: A pizza delivery driver and hacker uncovers a virus that can infect both computers and human minds in a corporate-run America.

title: The Stand
author: Stephen King
description: After a super-flu wipes out most of humanity, survivors are drawn to either a benevolent elder or a dark man in Las Vegas.

title: Ender's Game
author: Orson Scott Card
description: A brilliant child is recruited to a military academy in space to train for an alien war that may determine humanity's survival.

title: Slaughterhouse-Five
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: An optometrist becomes unstuck in time, reliving moments from World War II and his abduction by aliens on Tralfamadore.

title: The Martian
author: Andy Weir
description: An astronaut stranded on Mars must use his ingenuity to survive alone on the hostile planet until rescue can arrive.

title: Foundation
author: Isaac Asimov
description: A mathematician predicts the fall of a galactic empire and establishes a foundation to preserve knowledge through the coming dark age.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: A young nobleman navigates politics, religion, and ecology on a desert planet that holds the key to interstellar power.

title: The Hunger Games Trilogy
author: Suzanne Collins
description: The complete saga of rebellion against a tyrannical Capitol that forces children to fight to the death for entertainment.

title: Animal Farm / Nineteen Eighty-Four
author: George Orwell
description: Two masterworks of political fiction—an allegorical farm revolution and a nightmarish surveillance state—in one volume.

title: Never Let Me Go
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: Students at an idyllic English boarding school gradually discover the horrifying purpose for which they were created.

title: The Bachman Books
author: Stephen King
description: Four early novels including a deadly game show and a desperate man's run through a dystopian America.

title: The Walking Dead, Vol. 1
author: Robert Kirkman
description: A small-town sheriff wakes from a coma to find the world overrun by the undead and must lead survivors to safety.

title: The Coming Race
author: Edward Bulwer Lytton
description: An explorer discovers an advanced subterranean civilization powered by a mysterious energy called Vril.

title: Brave New World and Brave New World Revisited
author: Aldous Huxley
description: The classic dystopian novel paired with Huxley's nonfiction reassessment of how close his predictions came to reality.

title: Keeper of the Lost Cities Collection Books 1-5
author: Shannon Messenger
description: A telepathic girl discovers she belongs to a secret elven world and must navigate its dangers across five adventures.

title: Flowers for Algernon
author: Daniel Keyes
description: A mentally disabled man undergoes an experimental procedure that dramatically increases his intelligence—with devastating consequences.

title: The Circle
author: Dave Eggers
description: A young woman joins the world's most powerful tech company and watches as transparency becomes totalitarianism.

title: Station Eleven
author: Emily St. John Mandel
description: A traveling Shakespeare troupe performs for scattered settlements in the aftermath of a devastating flu pandemic.

title: The 5th Wave
author: Rick Yancey
description: After four waves of alien attacks devastate Earth, a teenager fights to save her brother from the mysterious fifth wave.

title: Oryx and Crake
author: Margaret Atwood
description: The last human survivor recalls the biotech catastrophe engineered by his brilliant, unhinged best friend.

title: The Windup Girl
author: Paolo Bacigalupi
description: In a post-oil Bangkok threatened by rising seas, an engineered human fights for survival in a world of corporate bioterror.

title: The Demolished Man
author: Alfred Bester
description: A businessman attempts to commit murder in a 24th-century society where telepathic police can read every thought.

title: The Chrysalids
author: John Wyndham
description: In a post-nuclear society that ruthlessly eliminates mutation, a group of telepathic children must hide their abilities to survive.

title: The Day of the Triffids
author: John Wyndham
description: Most of humanity is blinded by a meteor shower, leaving survivors to face carnivorous walking plants in a collapsing civilization.

title: Make Room! Make Room!
author: Harry Harrison
description: A detective investigates a murder in an overpopulated, resource-depleted New York City of 1999 in this ecological warning.

title: The Iron Heel
author: Jack London
description: A prophetic novel about the rise of a fascist oligarchy in America, told through a revolutionary's discovered manuscript.

title: The Long Walk
author: Stephen King
description: One hundred teenage boys enter a walking contest where the penalty for slowing down is death, and only one can win.

title: The Children of Men
author: P. D. James
description: In a world where humanity has become infertile, a disillusioned professor is drawn into a desperate mission to protect the last pregnancy.

title: The Trial
author: Franz Kafka
description: A man is arrested and prosecuted by an inscrutable authority for a crime that is never explained to him.

title: The Machine Stops
author: E. M. Forster
description: Humanity lives underground in isolated pods, entirely dependent on a global machine—until it begins to fail.

title: Logan's Run
author: William F. Nolan
description: In a society that executes everyone at age 21, an enforcer goes on the run when his own time comes.

title: The Lathe of Heaven
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
description: A man whose dreams can alter reality is exploited by a psychiatrist who tries to reshape the world for the better.

title: Stand on Zanzibar
author: John Brunner
description: An experimental novel depicting an overpopulated 2010 plagued by terrorism, genetic engineering, and corporate dominance.

title: The Sheep Look Up
author: John Brunner
description: Environmental catastrophe slowly engulfs America as pollution, contaminated food, and corporate greed spiral out of control.

title: A Canticle for Leibowitz
author: Walter M. Miller Jr.
description: Monks in a desert abbey preserve scientific knowledge through centuries of dark ages following nuclear war.

title: The Book of the New Sun
author: Gene Wolfe
description: A torturer's apprentice journeys across a dying Earth of the far future in this dense, literary science fantasy.

title: Kindred
author: Octavia E. Butler
description: A modern Black woman is repeatedly pulled back in time to a pre-Civil War plantation where she must ensure her own ancestor's survival.

title: The Stars My Destination
author: Alfred Bester
description: A castaway driven by revenge acquires the power of teleportation and tears through a future society to find those who abandoned him.

title: Cat's Cradle
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: A writer researching the atomic bomb discovers a substance that could freeze all the water on Earth in this satirical apocalypse.

title: The Testaments
author: Margaret Atwood
description: Three women's narratives converge to bring down the totalitarian regime of Gilead in this sequel to The Handmaid's Tale.

title: The City and the Stars
author: Arthur C. Clarke
description: A unique individual in a billion-year-old city breaks free to explore the mysteries of a long-forgotten Earth.

title: This Perfect Day
author: Ira Levin
description: A computer called UniComp controls every aspect of human life until one man dares to think and act independently.
